checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=701483000 (2008-12-31) rose to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=778786000 (2009-12-31), +11.0%, versus an 8.1% revenue decline

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=1070606000 (2007) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=872506000 (2007); NetIncomeLoss=1393153000 (2008) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1104568000 (2008) - NI exceeded OCF in both years by ~$200-290M
In 2009 the relationship reversed: NetIncomeLoss=253766000 (2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=614322000 (2009), OCF exceeding NI by more than 2x

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=2088342000 (2008-12-31) grew to InventoryNet=2239762000 (2009-12-31), +7.3%, while Revenues fell -8.1% over the same period
Gross margin compressed sharply: CostOfRevenue=7622015000 (2008) on Revenues=9600564000 (2008) (~20.6% margin) vs CostOfRevenue=8123078000 (2009) on Revenues=8826933000 (2009) (~8.0% margin)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss swung from 164802000 (Q1 2009) to 41981000 (Q2 2009) to 77374000 (Q3 2009), then to -40009000 (Q1 2010), -26564000 (Q2 2010), and 144846000 (Q3 2010) - highly volatile, not smoothed

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=16425000 (2008-12-31) grew to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=20466000 (2009-12-31), a +24.6% increase, outpacing the +2.5% growth in ReceivablesNetCurrent over the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-Q/A filed 2009-09-01 amending the Q2 2009 10-Q; 10-K/A filed 2010-04-30 amending the FY2009 10-K (originally filed 2010-03-01); 10-Q/A filed 2010-09-01 amending the Q2 2010 10-Q
Historical pattern of repeated annual report amendments: 10-K/A filed 2003-06-26, 10-K/A filed 2004-06-14, 10-K/A filed 2005-06-14

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Data spans annual figures 2006-2009 and quarterly figures through Q3 2010 (e.g., Revenues quarterly through 2010-07-01/2010-09-30=2534157000), providing multi-year trend visibility, though FY2010 full-year audited figures and post-cutoff filings are not yet available

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or delayed write-down of receivables during a period of declining top-line, evidenced by receivables continuing to grow (ReceivablesNetCurrent=2504876000 at 2009-12-31 vs 2442848000 at 2008-12-31, +2.5%) even as Revenues fell 8.1% (9600564000 in 2008 to 8826933000 in 2009), inconsistent with a shrinking business.

rationale_evidence.quote: Revenues=9600564000 (2008) to Revenues=8826933000 (2009); ReceivablesNetCurrent=2442848000 (2008-12-31) to ReceivablesNetCurrent=2504876000 (2009-12-31)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Q3 2010 comparative balance sheet / 10-K FY2009 income statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid goodwill build-up (+17.8% in a single quarter, Goodwill=3530915000 at 2008-12-31 to Goodwill=4159206000 at 2009-09-30) during a period of revenue contraction raises risk that acquisition-related goodwill/intangibles were not adequately tested for impairment, potentially overstating non-current assets.

affected_line_items: Income tax accounts (not directly itemized in provided data)
mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Chronic pattern of financial statement amendments (10-K/A FY2009 filed 2010-04-30; 10-Q/A for Q2 2009 filed 2009-09-01; 10-Q/A for Q2 2010 filed 2010-09-01) combined with unusually frequent SEC comment-letter correspondence (CORRESP/UPLOAD filings recurring through 2010-2011) suggests underlying control weaknesses, historically most often related to complex multinational tax/consolidation accounting, potentially understating tax liabilities and overstating reported net income in the periods covered.

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2010-04-30 (amending 10-K filed 2010-03-01); 10-Q/A filed 2009-09-01; 10-Q/A filed 2010-09-01; recurring CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2010-2011

top_signals: Chronic pattern of financial statement amendments (10-K/A, multiple 10-Q/A) plus heavy, recurring SEC comment-letter correspondence (CORRESP/UPLOAD) through 2010-2011, indicating persistent control/reporting weaknesses
Receivables grew (+2.5%) while revenue declined (-8.1%) in 2009, an inventory/receivable-to-revenue mismatch
Large single-quarter goodwill jump (+17.8%) amid revenue contraction, raising impairment/overstatement risk
Net income exceeded operating cash flow by wide margins in 2007-2008 before a sharp 2009 earnings collapse, indicating potential accrual-driven earnings quality issues
Inventory grew (+7.3%) and gross margin collapsed (~20.6% to ~8.0%) while revenue fell, consistent with either genuine downturn pressure or inadequate inventory reserve/cost absorption
